scenario.title: University controversy demo
scenario.subtitle: A precomputed, fixture-driven replay of the five-step go-mirofish flow.
scenario.seed_excerpt: A fictional university policy change triggers a wave of student, alumni, media, and administrator reactions across multiple social spaces.
scenario.prompt: Simulate how this fictional scenario evolves across public discussion, identify the key actors and likely sentiment shifts, and generate a concise analysis report.

full_flow.note: The local runtime stayed healthy; the external OpenAI-compatible provider exhausted quota during ontology generation.

steps.title: Graph Build
steps.summary: The seed document is parsed into actor and relationship candidates, then normalized into a graph-ready ontology.
bullets: Primary actor groups: students, alumni, media, university leadership.
Narrative anchors: policy reaction, trust erosion, and amplification pressure.
Graph construction is the first live stage affected by provider quota.

steps.title: Env Setup
steps.summary: The environment stage assembles agent personas, activity windows, posting rates, and event seeds for the simulation worlds.
bullets: Institutional accounts stay lower-volume but higher influence.
Student and alumni cohorts remain the most active amplifiers.
The static playground shows the expected configuration shape without spending live inference.

steps.title: Simulation
steps.summary: Dual-world replay shows how attention and sentiment move between feed-style and topic-style environments over time.
bullets: Rounds 1-2 are dominated by factual spread and institutional response.
Round 3 concentrates conflict around trust, transparency, and student voice.
In public production, this replay is static and free; real runs happen locally.

steps.title: Report
steps.summary: The report layer turns world-state evidence into an analyst-readable summary with causal signals and risk framing.
bullets: Student and alumni voices drive the fastest spread.
Institutional pacing affects whether the issue stabilizes or escalates.
Media participation extends the lifetime of the discussion.

steps.title: Deep Interaction
steps.summary: The playground replays a few fixed interactions so visitors can inspect the interface without spending your inference budget.
bullets: "As an alum, the issue feels bigger than the policy itself; it is about whether leadership listens at all."
"Institutional posts reduce uncertainty only when they arrive before rumor hardens."
"A replayed demo keeps the public playground interactive while pushing real compute to local/self-hosted mode."
